text.title: PMBoK 8 Six Core Principles Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the six-core-principles methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: 'Passed: true' requires positive evidence quoting the artefact; absence of failure is not a pass.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to all-green outputs because they want to be helpful; forced evidence prevents silent compliance inflation.

rule.statement: Any principle that fails must block the stage gate; the artefact must be fixed or escalated before review concludes.
rule.rationale: Audit without a rejection consequence is theatre.

rule.statement: Use exactly the six canonical names when auditing: Adopt Holistic View, Focus on Value, Embed Quality, Lead Accountably, Integrate Sustainability, Build Empowered Teams.
rule.rationale: Renamed principles produce inconsistent diffs across teams.

rule.statement: Integrate Sustainability requires a numeric proxy (CO2 estimate, accessibility WCAG level, social-impact scope statement); narrative does not pass.
rule.rationale: Narrative sustainability is unmeasurable; numeric proxies enable progress tracking.

rule.statement: Lock the PMBoK edition ('PMBoK 8, six principles') in every system prompt that runs a principle audit; never let the agent infer the edition.
rule.rationale: LLMs conflate PMBoK 7 twelve principles with PMBoK 8 six; edition ambiguity produces incorrect audits.

list.item: Tactical execution work (sprint planning, ticket triage) — principles too abstract
Non-PMI environments (PRINCE2, IPMA, SAFe) — vocabulary clashes
Audit / compliance requiring named standard (ISO 21500, 21502) — principles are guidance
Technical deep-dives (architecture, security) — principles do not substitute for expertise
